taglines: Ten years. One kitchen table. Here's the shape it made.
Hazel is sixteen. Bram is thirteen. Look what they made of you.
Well — did any of it stick?
You taught the math, mostly. They taught you the rest.
Some of the lessons hit. The kids remember the other ones.
Ten years of small choices. They added up.
You were the teacher, the cook, the schedule. Mostly the schedule.
Most of it was Tuesdays. Tuesdays do most of the work.
The kitchen table held everything you put on it.
You showed up. Every day. That was most of it.
Look at them now. Look at you.
It was not what you imagined. It was the thing instead.
